why: Bilingual (Nepali + English) QA dataset grounded in primary Nepali law texts with explicit citations — ideal for RAG fine-tuning and improving grounded answer generation and bilingual alignment. Adds legal specificity and precise source grounding missing from other candidates.
warnings: License: cc-by-sa-4.0 (share-alike) — imposes reuse requirements; confirm compatibility with your training/distribution plan.
Relatively small (4.8K rows) — high quality but insufficient alone for post-training; needs to be combined with other corpora.
Verify citation grounding and run spot checks for OCR/formatting issues in source passages.

why: Nepali-language QA focused on Nepal legal framework (rights of persons with disabilities) and produced from official sources — provides domain-relevant Nepali raw text and question–answer pairs to bias the model toward Nepali legal phrasing and domain coverage.
warnings: License: apache-2.0 — permissive, but confirm metadata/source licensing for included source documents.
May contain domain-specific narrow coverage (disability rights); you should supplement for broader legal coverage.
Check for Devanagari encoding inconsistencies and OCR/noise; consider script normalization and PII redaction.

why: Additional Nepali QA pairs that increase Nepali-language coverage and diversity of question styles and sources; Apache-2.0 license is permissive and useful for fine-tuning and evaluation.
warnings: Dataset size (~11K rows) helps increase row count but combined selection remains below the target 50K+ rows for robust post-training.
Potential overlap with other QA sets — deduplicate and validate content quality before weighting heavily.
Check for romanized Nepali, mixed-script entries, and PII in judgments or examples.
